mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as cost of capital falls.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals imminent corporate earnings deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter as revenue growth expectations rise.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks and fading economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration during late-cycle phases yields positive 20-day forward returns in >58% of cases, or regime-specific IC turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate margins and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns, or the correlation coefficient exceeds +0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent quarterly, robust aggregate demand supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 60-day returns with >55% accuracy, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for market valuation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total employment and falling unemployment confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns, or the joint signal yields negative returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation environments, elevated federal funds rates restrict liquidity and increase borrowing costs, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >57% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210 and FEDFUNDS is elevated, or the regime-specific predictive IC falls below zero.
